Company DescriptionIt all started in sunny San Diego, California in 2004 when a visionary engineer, Fred Luddy, saw the potential to transform how we work. Fast forward to today — ServiceNow stands as a global market leader, bringing innovative AI-enhanced technology to over 8,100 customers, including 85% of the Fortune 500®. Our intelligent cloud-based platform seamlessly connects people, systems, and processes to empower organizations to find smarter, faster, and better ways to work. But this is just the beginning of our journey. Join us as we pursue our purpose to make the world work better for everyone.Job DescriptionWe are looking for a Global Executive Marketing Apprentice to join our Global Executive Marketing (GEM) team, based in the UK.The Global Executive Marketing team focuses on engaging senior C-suite leaders from the world’s largest organisations. Our mission is to build meaningful executive relationships by combining world-class storytelling, digital thought leadership, and curated in-person experiences that position ServiceNow as a trusted partner in enterprise transformation.You don’t need prior experience in executive marketing — we’ll help you learn what makes this audience unique and how to engage them effectively.As an apprentice, you’ll gain exposure to global marketing initiatives while completing an accredited qualification through our apprentice provider, Apprentify, who will support your technical development and ensure you complete the relevant modules.About the Global Executive Marketing TeamThe Global Executive Marketing team operates at the intersection of strategy, storytelling, and experience design. We partner with senior leaders across the business to:Deliver high-impact executive experiences at major global events and curated roundtablesDevelop C-suite thought leadership and digital storytelling programsSupport relationship building within our most strategic global accountsEnsure executive engagement translates into measurable business impactThis team collaborates across sales, solution consulting, corporate marketing, regional field marketing teams, and external partners. While we work globally, this role will be UK-based and support both regional and international initiatives.What You’ll Learn and Experience:Depending on the projects you support, you’ll gain hands-on experience in:Executive Events & ExperiencesSupporting the delivery of curated executive roundtables and leadership eventsAssisting with event logistics, communications, and follow-up campaignsHelping create executive briefing materials and on-the-day experiencesUnderstanding how relationship-led marketing differs from traditional demand generationYou’ll see firsthand how executive engagement is designed to deepen relationships and influence strategic business outcomes.Executive Storytelling & Thought LeadershipSupporting the development of C-suite-level content and messagingHelping coordinate digital storytelling initiatives and executive campaignsLearning how marketing speaks differently to CIOs, CFOs, CHROs, and other C-suite leadersUnderstanding how thought leadership builds credibility at the highest levelsStrategy, Measurement & InsightsSupporting audience research and executive account profilingLearning how executive marketing impact is measuredAssisting with tracking engagement metrics and pipeline influenceGaining exposure to account-based marketing and strategic account planningMarketing Technology & OperationsGaining experience with marketing and CRM platformsSupporting campaign setup, invitation management, and reportingUnderstanding how marketing automation and executive outreach integrateCollaboration & Stakeholder ManagementWorking with senior internal stakeholdersDeveloping professional communication skillsLearning how to manage multiple workstreams and prioritiesQualificationsWho you are:For us, attitude and behaviours matter most. We can teach executive marketing skills, but we can’t teach curiosity, accountability, composure, and collaboration.We’re looking for someone who:Is proactive, curious, and eager to learnIs comfortable working in a fast-paced, professional environmentHas strong written and verbal communication skillsIs organised and detail-orientedIs open to feedback and willing to growWorks well in a team and builds positive relationshipsIs confident communicating with a wide range of stakeholdersYou do not need prior marketing experience — that’s what the apprenticeship is for. Any exposure to marketing, events, communications, or business studies is a bonus but not required.Fluency in English is required. Additional language skills are a bonus given the global nature of the team.This role requires attendance at our Staines office a minimum of 3 days per week.Additional InformationWork PersonasWe approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. We don’t expect you to know the difference between those area’s as yet and help you navigate the most suitable role during the hiring process.Our EMEA Strategic Events team is responsible for delivering key big rock events throughout the year to support the EMEA-wide region. Our goal is to deliver meaningful and impactful experiences for our customers which in return creates opportunity or accelerates or influences pipeline. We cover a wide range of experiences including ServiceNow led events, third party activations and our workshop program. We partner across the organisation across multiple teams to build programs to support our business priorities.The Solution Marketing team is responsible for developing the Go-to-Market strategy and executing EMEA wide campaigns to generate sales pipelines for the wider business. They work in partnership with Solution Sales and Solution Consulting to ensure the EMEA marketing guidance and campaigns deliver for the local business needs.Our UK&I Marketing team’s ultimate goal is to establish a connection with customers and prospects, in order to help them understand and discover more about what ServiceNow could do for them. Their intern is to help our Sales team to create opportunities and then influence and accelerate them to close deals and help hit revenue goals.Whilst there’s a lot of cross over in these teams, the work will be different across all of them and partner with different internal stakeholders along the way.As an Apprentice in these teams, you’ll have the chance to learn about and support the implementation of the key activities that goes into the different and varied activities. You’ll be learning alongside completing an accredited qualification via our apprentice provider, Apprentify who will support you with technical skills and ensure you’re completing the relevant modules.Some of the things you might learn (depending on the team you’re working with):Whether it’s a client who has never heard of ServiceNow or our most loyal customers, you will gain experience in marketing to each audience and learn how to position ServiceNow and our products to best position usWhile what we do is so much more than events, there is a special buzz that comes from seeing customers attend and enjoy what you’ve created, and you’ll experience exactly that! Creating registration pages, promoting the events, crafting the on-the-day experience and building follow up campaigns – you'll be part of the team that makes it all happen!Stretch your data analytics skills by considering our audiences, revenue targets and metrics from previous campaigns and what we need to do to make the most of both whilst learning how to measure the outcomes too.You’ll build your internal communications skills as you develop your understanding of what your key stakeholders need from you and the team.Gain experience with marketing-tech (martech) tools and apps such as our own CRM, Folloze, Swoogo, Marketo (via our corporate colleagues) and others and how to best use them to yours and ServiceNow’s benefit.You’ll learn how multiple aspects of marketing come together to drive business results be it branding, communications, demand generation, partner marketing, Account Based Marketing etc.How to develop custom marketing strategies to help land specific clients having researched everything you can find out about them in order to better understand the opportunity in front of youQualificationsWho you are:For us attitude and behavior's are all important – we can teach you everything you need to know from a technical skills perspective, but we can’t teach attitude or how you approach your work – be open to feedback, be easy to collaborate with in your teams and show a desire to learn and grow.Curiosity and pro-activity will take you a long way.Good verbal and written communication is crucial as you’ll be dealing with lots of internal stakeholders in other teams and also external clients, therefore being clear, concise and helping others understand what is expected of them is vitalYou don’t need any experience in Marketing, although if you do that’s a bonus, but between us and Apprentify, you’ll learn everything you need to know about how to do the job.Side hustles are always viewed favorably.Fluent in English and if you do speak another European language that will come in handy too.This role requires attendance at our Staines office a minimum of 3 days per week.Additional InformationWork PersonasWe approach our distributed world of work with job_name: Staff Inbound Product Manager - CRM Contact Center Omni-Channel - Workforce Engagement Management
job_desc: Company DescriptionCompany:ServiceNow is making the world of work, work better for people. Our cloud‑based platform and solutions deliver digital workflows that create great experiences and unlock productivity for employees and the enterprise. We're growing fast, innovating faster, and making an impact on our customers' and employees' lives in significant and important ways. With over 6,900 customers, we serve approximately 80% of the Fortune 500, and we're on the 2020 list of FORTUNE World's Most Admired Companies.®We’re looking for people who are ready to jump right in and help us build on our incredible momentum, our diverse, engaged workforce, and our purpose to make the world of work, work better.Learn more onLife at Now blogandhear from our employeesabout their experiences working at ServiceNow.Team:ServiceNow’s Customer Service Management team is transforming customer experience by enabling digitization of customer workflows across the front, middle and back office in a seamless and connected manner. We are enabling our customers to accelerate their digital transformation by providing end to end solutions that are as much focused on providing a consumer grade experience to agents as to end users. Our customers span across industries and our goal is to build horizontal Customer Engagement capabilities that plug-n-play and adapt to needs of the domains. The team consists of experienced and passionate product managers who have deep domain knowledge of customer experience technologies and help us deliver best of breed engagement solutions.Job DescriptionRole:We are executing a holistic Customer Engagement strategy to deliver best‑in‑class outcomes for customers and agents. As a Principal Product Manager (IC4) for Workforce Engagement Management (WEM) in the CRM Omnichannel Contact Center domain, you will own product strategy and execution across the following capability areas:Scheduling & Forecasting (WFM)Coaching & Skill ManagementReal‑time Monitoring of Channels (intraday operations)Monitoring & Managing Team Performance (analytics + execution loops)Product managers at ServiceNow operate at the intersection of market opportunity, customer experience design, technology development, and delivering value to our customers—now increasingly via governed AI and agentic workflows embedded directly inside work.This role requires a unique blend of customer empathy, operational rigor, strong product craftsmanship, and AI‑driven product judgment. Modern WEM is being reshaped by AI—forecasting and staffing must increasingly account for digital/AI deflection and bot‑to‑human escalations, and supervisors need real‑time guidance to respond to volatility across channels.Product Managers at ServiceNow usually are obsessed about their products/domains, often geek out on technology and are catalysts for innovation.What you get to do in this roleOwn product strategy and execution for Workforce Engagement Management (WEM) capabilities in CRM omnichannel contact centers, with primary focus on scheduling & forecasting, real‑time channel monitoring, coaching & skill management, and team performance management.Define and deliver AI‑driven forecasting and scheduling experiences across voice and digital channels, including demand forecasting, schedule optimization, intraday management, and real‑time adjustments in response to volume variability.Build real‑time operational monitoring capabilities that provide supervisors with actionable visibility into queues, SLAs, adherence, capacity, and service risk across channels.Design coaching and skill management workflows that connect performance signals to targeted coaching actions, skill development, and measurable improvement for agents and teams.Deliver team performance management experiences that balance customer outcomes, agent experience, and operational efficiency through role‑based dashboards, insights, and guided actions.Partner closely with UX, engineering, and data/AI teams to ship scalable, enterprise‑ready WEM capabilities with strong product craftsmanship and reliability at scale.Apply sound AI product judgment, ensuring transparency, explainability, and human‑in‑the‑loop controls for AI‑assisted recommendations and automation.Engage directly with enterprise customers to validate requirements, drive adoption, and ensure measurable business outcomes from deployed WEM capabilities.QualificationsTo succeed in this role, we seek someone who has:8+ years of experience building and delivering enterprise or SaaS products, with at least 4+ years in a product management role owning end‑to‑end execution.Proven experience building and deploying Workforce Engagement Management (WEM) capabilities for contact centers within a CRM omnichannel environment.Strong hands‑on expertise across scheduling and forecasting, real‑time monitoring of channels, coaching and skill management, and team performance management for contact center operations.Deep understanding of contact center operations, including omnichannel demand patterns, workforce constraints, supervisor workflows, and operational KPIs.Demonstrated experience working closely with UX, engineering, and data/AI teams to deliver scalable, enterprise‑ready capabilities.Strong product judgment when applying AI‑driven solutions, with focus on explainability, trust, and human‑in‑the‑loop decision making.Ability to work directly with enterprise customers to gather requirements, validate solutions, and drive the adoption of complex operational products.Experience in working directly with Customers, Sales, and Engineering, preferably in North America or EuropeExcellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate product decisions to stakeholders at multiple levels.High energy, self-starter with an aptitude for learning new technologiesPrevious experience as an engineer in development, quality assurance, or DevOps/CloudOps during the early stages of one's career is considered advantageous.Additional InformationWork PersonasWe approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust.
